target.title: fix(agents): keep lightweight cron prompts out of no-op compaction
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #96658.

Lightweight isolated cron runs on very small context windows can reserve too much of the model window for response headroom before the first prompt is sent. In the reported 4,096-token model case, current `main` leaves only 2,048 prompt tokens for a 3,544-token lightweight cron prompt, so the precheck routes to `compact_only` even though lightweight cron has no bootstrap history to compact.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the shared small-context prompt floor conservative: `MIN_PROMPT_BUDGET_RATIO` remains `0.5` for full-context callers, settings reserve capping, CLI compaction, mid-turn guard paths, and lightweight shared-history runs such as non-isolated heartbeats.

The higher 87.5% prompt share is now scoped to history-free lightweight pre-prompt admission only. The precheck also subtracts estimated tool-schema pressure before accepting `fits`, using the provider-visible tool surface from both runtime tools and client-supplied tool definitions.

`toolSchemaTokens` is diagnostic-only and optional on `PreemptiveCompactionDecision`, preserving the public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime` type shape for existing SDK consumers.

## User Impact

Lightweight cron jobs on small-context fallback models can proceed when the rendered prompt plus tool schema surface fits the model window, instead of failing through repeated compact-only recovery with nothing useful to compact.

Tool-heavy small-context runs and shared-history lightweight runs remain conservative, and non-lightweight/full-context paths keep the previous shared reserve behavior.

## Evidence

Earlier real lightweight cron behavior proof on this PR branch:

```text
Setup: source Gateway from this PR branch, temp HOME and OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR, local mock OpenAI Responses server.
Config: openai/gpt-5.5 contextWindow=4096 contextTokens=4096, agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokens=20000.
Action: cron.add with sessionTarget=isolated, payload.kind=agentTurn, payload.lightContext=true, then cron.run mode=force.
Result: cron finished status=ok; mock provider received 2 POST /v1/responses requests.
```

Gateway debug proof from that run:

```text
[context-overflow-precheck] pre-prompt check ... provider=openai/gpt-5.5 route=fits estimatedPromptTokens=3318 pressureSource=llm_boundary_normalized_prompt promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584 overflowTokens=0 toolResultReducibleChars=0 reserveTokens=20000 effectiveReserveTokens=512 contextTokenBudget=4096 messages=0 unwindowedMessages=0 sessionFile=<tmp-state>/agents/main/sessions/<session>.jsonl
```

Current-head boundary proof for the reported 4,096-token / 20,000-reserve / 3,544-token case:

```text
history-free lightweight, no tools: route=fits, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3584, toolSchemaTokens=0, effectiveReserveTokens=512
history-free lightweight, 570-token schema: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=3014, overflowTokens=530
lightweight with shared history: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
full mode, no tools: route=compact_only, promptBudgetBeforeReserve=2048
```

Red test before the original fix:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts
AssertionError: expected 2048 to be greater than or equal to 3544
```

Focused validation on current head `26ea90c853a`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
# PASS 39 tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.test.ts
# PASS 16 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# PASS

node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/preemptive-compaction.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts
# PASS

git diff --check
# PASS
```

Review comments addressed:

- Codex's original tool-schema budgeting concern: addressed by subtracting tool schema pressure before history-free lightweight `fits` admission.
- Codex's client-supplied tools follow-up: addressed by budgeting `effectiveTools` plus `clientToolDefs`, matching the provider-visible custom tool surface used for the session.
- Codex's heartbeat/shared-history follow-up: addressed by keeping lightweight runs with any history on the shared 2,048-token floor for 4k models.
- ClawSweeper's SDK compatibility finding: addressed by making `toolSchemaTokens` optional and adding a plugin SDK type compatibility test that constructs the old decision shape.

Autoreview: attempted one local draft-PR review round, but the helper failed before review due local Codex config parsing (`service_tier = priority`; helper expected `fast` or `flex`). No review findings were produced.

Agent transcript: a matching local session was found, but no transcript is included because adding sanitized transcript logs requires explicit approval.
target.author: monkeyleet
